description: Demand-elasticity sweep on the recommended calibration (both-dynamic-strict-threshold, window 5, target 0.5, max-change denominator 16). The stability envelope was validated at one demand elasticity; this sweep varies it: all10x scales every actor's values 10x (demand sheds ~10x later), hv10/hv25 add a bounded-inelastic tranche (10%/25% of arrivals at 100x values - very high but finite willingness to pay), and mech-hv25-d8 runs the harshest mix at denominator 8 to check the envelope's D range under steep demand. Each mix carries its own flat-fee control because the mix changes offered value: paired deltas must compare like against like. The base mix's pair (bdst-tu50-d16, flat-fee) is included for within-sweep anchoring. Run under severe-congestion and launch-day profiles with matching --out directories
